summary: Paucigranulocytic asthmatics exhibit underlying eosinophilic inflammation despite low or absent neutrophil presence; thus, they remain clinically significant for type 2 inflammatory pathways rather than being genuinely non-inflamed. This challenges the notion that their lack of visible granules equates to absence of immune-mediated disease activity.
